selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As an 18-year-old student in Kansas City who uses social media a few times weekly, I've seen how fake content can spread quickly, especially among my peers. My creative nature makes me curious about why people create misinformation, but my high conscientiousness pushes me to verify what I see before sharing. With high neuroticism, I often feel anxious when encountering conflicting information online, worrying about being misled. My moderate agreeableness means I'm not overly trusting, so I question sensational posts. To manage fake content, I start by checking sources—looking for credible websites or official accounts. I also use fact-checking tools like Snopes or media literacy resources from my school. Since I'm extraverted, I discuss posts with friends during runs or cooking sessions, which helps us spot inconsistencies together. My Hindu beliefs emphasize truthfulness, guiding me to avoid spreading falsehoods. I limit my social media time to reduce exposure, focusing instead on hobbies like cycling to stay grounded. Platforms should improve algorithms to flag fake content and educate users, especially students like me. Ultimately, being proactive—pausing before reacting, educating myself, and fostering open conversations—helps me navigate this digital landscape responsibly.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 18 and student status imply engagement with peer social media trends; creative psychological feature suggests curiosity about misinformation origins; high conscientiousness (score 71) drives verification habits; high neuroticism (score 97) explains anxiety around fake content; moderate agreeableness (score 47) indicates skepticism; extraversion (score 74) supports collaborative fact-checking with friends; Hinduism influences ethical stance against falsehoods; time on social media (few times weekly) shapes practical management strategies; hobbies provide offline balance. Tone is personal and reflective, blending youthful concern with proactive solutions, avoiding bullet points as specified.
